walls.ex: 玉器（常設與特展）
walls.section: 玉器展廳（寫實與辟邪 / 動物形玉雕）
walls.text: 寫實與辟邪 商晚期至西周，用閃玉製作的動物形立雕，多為體積不大的厚片或圓柱、方柱體。東周時，鐵工具漸普及，提升了琢玉技術。不但作品體積增大，動物的頭、尾、肢體也不再侷限於固定的塊體內，有了動作與神情。有的較為寫實，也有的增添了象徵神性的冠、角、翼、牙等，以為能辟邪致福。小件立雕常帶有穿孔，可穿繩繫掛。 Realism & Mysticism The animal-shape nephrite carvings from late Shang to Western Zhou dynasties were generally small and came in blocks, cylinders or cuboids. As the spread of iron tools during the Eastern Zhou period helped upgrade the skills of jade artisans, their works grew in size and no longer confined the subject animal's head, tail, and body into one single space; each part started to exhibit individual postures and expressions. Some of these animal carvings were more realistic and others were endowed with crowns, horns, wings, or fangs symbolic of divine power for blessing and bixie (warding off evils). Smaller works in the round often came with string holes for wearing or hanging.

walls.ex: 玉器（常設與特展）
walls.text: 玉之德 The Virtue of Jade 西元前2000年至西元581年 circa 2000 B.C.E. to 581 C.E. 西元前2000至西元581年，也就是距今約四千年至一千四百多年前，共計約二千五百餘年。漫長的歲月歷經了夏、商、周、秦、漢、魏晉南北朝，共六個階段。若不計國祚甚短的秦，與基本上呈現分裂的魏晉南北朝，主要的四個朝代：夏、商、周、漢，王室輪流來自華西與華東。雙方勢力輪替消長，文化漸進融合，到漢代時已大致融為一體，開啟統一的新局面。在此過程裡，遠古先民迷信美玉具有特殊「靈性」的思維，隨著社會進步，人文主義抬頭，儒家學說興起而逐漸被道德化。 「德性」一詞最初的含意是指「天生秉賦」，無關乎善惡與道德。在遠古時，人們相信上帝（周代開始稱為「天」）派遣神靈動物將生命力賜予氏族的始祖。因此配戴各式動物主題的玉飾，除可結合美玉的「精氣」與動物的「法力」來溝通人神外，更能彰顯自身承襲的神靈秉賦，也就是「德性」；但是到了東周時，人們已淡忘戴玉的最初意義，儒家則以比較理性的態度，解釋美玉具有各種特性，正象徵「君子」具有仁、義、智、勇、絜等美德。「君子」一詞的原意本指「統治者」，東周時也在儒家的理論體系中轉化為「品德高尚的知識份子」。 在此悠久的歲月裡，周人所施行的「圭璧組配」成為日後中國玉禮制的核心；漢代皇室來自沛縣，江南古越俗的「玉殮葬」更發展達於極致。源自域外的辟邪、角杯傳到中土後，不但用美玉雕琢，更增添華夏的神秘元素。

walls.ex: 玉器（常設與特展）
walls.text: 漫長的新石器時代，鷹、梟、鳩、燕，各種「玄鳥」信仰流傳於夷、越族群中，但到了約距今四千年前後，尖喙長尾的玉鳳出現了。祂優雅的造形可能來自孔雀與長尾雉。長江中游的石家河文化晚期遺址出土了完整的玉鳳，而本院展出的山東龍山文化玉圭上淺浮雕大眼的神祖面，「介」字形冠頂被高高抬起，兩側插飾飄逸的鳳羽。 During the l... existed in various... turtledoves, and s... and Yue. Then a... sharp-beaked an... emerged; the ele... from peacocks or... of late Shijiahe... reach of Yangzi... condition were u... gui tablet came... ture, featuring a... eyes in low relie... flaunting gracef...

walls.ex: 玉器（常設與特展）
walls.text: 「神主」與「鳩杖」 「神主」就是神祖牌位。從石家河文化晚期到西周早期（約西元前2000-977年），先民用美玉製作或雕有面紋、或素色的「神主」，後者上面有時寫有祖先名號。商族屬東夷族系，將雕有玄鳥紋飾的玉飾插嵌於長杆上，祭典中用以依附自天而降的神祖之靈。發展到漢代，流行象徵王庇佑眾生的「鳩杖」。 Ancestral Tablet & Turtledove Staff Shenzhu (ancestral tablet) is a plaque symbolizing a specific ancestor. From the late Shijiahe Culture to early Western Zhou dynasty (c. 2000-977 B.C.E.), people made jade tablets which were either carved with patterns or plain but inscribed with the deceased ancestor's name. The Shang people, of Yi clan in the east, in ritual ceremonies used long wood poles top-set with jade ornaments of black bird designs for ancestral spirits to descend upon. During Han dynasty such poles developed into staffs topped with a turtledove, signifying the imperial benevolent protection for all his subjects.

walls.ex: 玉器（常設與特展）
walls.text: 「圭璧制度」的確立 西周時，高級貴族墓葬中常將大玉戈疊在玉璧上，構成一組「圭璧」，放在墓主胸前或棺蓋上，所用之戈、璧，不乏前代古物。東周以降，大玉戈逐漸演變成無中脊、邊刃，也無刃部、柄部之分的尖首圭。除可與玉璧成組使用外，周、漢之時也曾與斜刃者組成所謂「圭璋」。 The Establishment of the Gui-Bi Ritual During Western Zhou dynasty, deceased high aristocrats were often buried with a large jade ge dagger place over a jade bi disc. The set, very often antiques from even earlier periods, was placed either upon his chest or the cover of his coffin. During Eastern Zhou dynasty, such large daggers gradually evolved into gui tablets with a double-bevel tip, with neither spine nor side edges, and without clear demarcation of the blade section from the handle. In addition to forming a Gui-and-Bi set, gui was also used during the 8th to 2nd centuries B.C.E. in combination with single-bevel-blade tablets as a Gui-and-Zhang set.

walls.ex: 玉器（常設與特展）
walls.section: 玉器展廳-玉之德/玉璧區
walls.text: 法力無邊的玉璧 玉璧是禮制地位最高的玉器。祭祀之外，還用於招賢納聘及元旦朝賀等儀式中。喪禮中玉璧用以裹尸，綴連在全套玉匣包裹在人頭頂的部位，或釘於棺材頭端，希望引領魂靈通過玉璧中孔，達到「太一」之境。戰國至漢代玉璧上的紋飾有：雲紋、勾連雲紋、穀紋、蒲紋、乳丁紋等。有的還雕琢龍鳳紋，四神紋與吉祥語。 The All-Powerful Jade Bi Disc The Jade bi disc was the top article in the ritual hierarchy. It was used in sacred worship as well as secular ceremonies, such as in recruiting talent, proposing a marriage, betrothal gifts, new year audience and celebrations at the court, and so on. A funerary suit sewn of jade pieces would wrap the deceased body, with a bi disc specifically for the apex of the head or the head end of the coffin, so the soul would hopefully pass through the central hole of the disc into eternity. The patterns that adorn the jade discs from the Warring States Period to Han dynasty include designs in cloud, cloud scroll, grain, rush mat, embossment, and sometimes with motifs of dragon-phoenix or four-deity, and auspicious phrases.

walls.ex: 玉器（常設與特展）
walls.text: 嘉慶時的玉器風格與乾隆相去不遠，只是製作數量大減，這種情況到道光時更形明顯。此外，輝玉的使用率增加。 The styles of jades made under Emperor Jiaqing were close to those of the Qianlong reign but in much less quantity. The downturn was ever more apparent when Daoguang was on the throne. Usage of jadeite on the other hand was on the rise.

walls.ex: 玉器（常設與特展）
walls.section: 玉器展廳-清代/民國玉器區
walls.text: 兩只花鳥紋玉瓶是汪精衛於民國三十年贈與日本天皇家族的禮物，均高浮雕長尾的綬帶鳥，寓意長壽。 Both jade vases with flower and bird patterns were gifts from Wang Jingwei to the imperial family of Japan in 1941. The paradise flycatchers, carved in high relief, symbolize longevity.

walls.ex: 玉器（常設與特展）
walls.text: 五彩繽紛的石英家族 The Colorful Quartz Family 自然界中最容易被發現的美石是石英家族。石英的成分是二氧化矽，又依其晶粒是否能在顯微鏡下被看到，而分為顯晶質與隱晶質（或稱：粗晶與微晶）兩類，可分別稱作水晶與瑪瑙。玉髓因為帶有各種致色色素與內含物等因素，會呈現不同的顏色與外觀。玉髓若帶有條紋、紋狀色斑，就是國人熟悉的瑪瑙。中國境內蘊藏的瑪瑙多又分佈甚廣，距今約五千多年，長江下游的北陰陽營文化、凌家灘文化中，頗多用玉髓、瑪瑙製作的玦飾、璜等裝飾品，甚至還用瑪瑙製作斧頭。在西周時，突然大量出現全紅色的玉髓珠、管，到也玉髓、閃玉等串連成美麗的組珮，本院這類藏品展於「玉之德」單元。 文獻記載，自古就從西域傳入各種瑪瑙，所以，很難從瑪瑙的色澤文理推測其產地。通常色帶平行直線的稱「縞瑪瑙」，紋理細密層層纏繞的稱為「纏絲瑪瑙」，似綠繞瑪瑙就稱作「苔蘚瑪瑙」和「柏枝瑪瑙」。玉髓若含有銅、鎳、鐵等致色色素會呈現藍色、綠色與黃色。前二種本院藏品中沒有見到。院藏名品「三連章」曾被訂名為「黃玉」，距今多年前經比重測定，改名為「黃玉髓」；近日再測得以折射指數與拉曼光譜，更確定其質地為玉髓。完全不透明的玉髓稱為碧石，本院有名的肉形石就是顏色呈帶狀的碧石。 透明無色的水晶中，若含有三價鐵離子就會呈現黃色或紫色。若含有鈦離子，就會呈現粉紅色。若含有鋁離子，且受到微量輻射，破壞了透明水晶的原子結構，就會變成煙水晶（或稱為茶晶），如果太濃就成了墨晶。至於本院有名的髮晶雕馬背猴，就是水晶內包裹了金紅石或電氣石的針狀晶體。內部裂璺等的粉晶、紫晶、煙晶等，在清宮中常被稱作「碧霞」。此間第1至4櫃展出五彩繽紛的石英家族，第5櫃將粉晶、紫晶並列。至於帳冊舊名為「綠水晶」者，近日經測折射指數與拉曼光譜，確定為與祖母綠同一家族的海藍寶石，故與嵌有祖母綠的水晶罐併陳於第9櫃。

walls.ex: 玉器（常設與特展）
walls.text: The Quartz family boasts beautiful stones that can be most easily found in nature. Mainly consisting in silica, texturally it can be either phaneritic (macrocrystalline) or aphanitic (microcrystalline), and commonly categorized as rock crystal and chalcedony, respectively. Various colorants and inclusions also give quartz different hues and appearances, among which agate is a familiar type of chalcedony featuring colors in bands or masses. China is rich in chalcedony deposits, which are found in many areas across the country. The Beiyinyangying Culture and Linjiatan Culture, both in the lower reach of Yangzi River and dated more than 5000 years ago, had many jue earrings and huang ornaments made of chalcedony. There were even agate fu axes. Then in Western Zhou dynasty, all-red chalcedony beads and tubes suddenly appeared in large quantity; they were threaded with other types of chalcedony as well as nephrite to compose beautiful sets of pei ornaments in pendant style, featuring in the "Virtue of Jade" section of the exhibition. Literature records show that agate had been imported from Western Regions to China since the remote past. It is difficult to infer the place of origin for a given piece of agate based on its colors and textures. There are several types of agate: onyx, parallel striped; sardonyx, tightly entwined; moss agate, with chlorite inclusions; dentritic agate, with pyrolusite inclusions. Chalcedony with colorant contents such as copper, nickel or iron would appear blue, green, and yellow. The former two are not seen in the Museum collection, whereas the renowned Tri-Seal set was once named as yellow "jade" and have since reclassified as yellow "chalcedony" after a density test, confirmed again recently using the refractive index and Raman spectroscopy. Chalcedony of total opacity is called jasper; the famous meat-shaped stone is of a jasper with banded colors. Transparent, colorless rock crystals would become tinted by the presence of different ions: ferric iron, yellow or violet; titanium, pink. With aluminum ions, and if exposed to trace radiation altering the crystalline structure, the color would change to grayish brown, called smoky quartz (or brown quartz), or even to darker shades under higher concentration. The well-known carving of monkey riding horse in the Museum collection is a rock crystal of acicular prism included with rutile or tourmaline. Rose quartz, amethyst, and smoky quartz with many inner crack lines were often called "tourmaline" in the Qing court. The cabinets 1 to 4 show the specimens from the colorful quartz family, and cabinet 5 juxtaposes a rose quartz and a tourmaline for comparison. So-called "green crystal" as recorded in the old palace catalogues has been reclassified by the refractive index and Raman spectroscopy as aquamarine, belonging in the same category as emerald. Therefore it is placed in the same cabinet (9) with the emerald-inlaid crystal jar.

walls.ex: 玲瓏璀璨｜寶石與珠寶特展
walls.section: 玲瓏璀璨：寶石與珠寶特展
walls.text: 玲瓏璀璨 Dazzling Gemstones and Jewelry 在中國雖然有著長達七、八千年製作與使用玉器的歷史，但在十八世紀以前，先民所採用的玉料主要是閃玉；常見的顏色爲白、青、灰、黑、黃、綠，若有大量三價鐵滲入，就會變成褐紅色。 蘊藏在北緬甸的輝玉，雖有近五百年的開採史，但直到近三百年前的十八世紀，該地區歸屬清廷雲南省管轄時，所產的輝玉才逐漸大量輸入中土；因爲它鮮豔的翡紅與翠綠就像翠鳥的羽毛色澤，因此被稱爲「翡翠玉」有。 閃玉與輝玉雖分屬不同的礦物家族，卻有相似的外觀。它們都可呈現美麗的綠色，但因致色元素分別爲鐵與鉻，所以，閃玉多介於草綠至碧綠，輝玉則是亮麗的翠綠。一般稱前者爲「碧玉」，後者爲「翠玉」。但後者若由有微量二價鐵存在，原有的翠綠就易略偏暗草綠。而品質優良的綠色閃玉，也常煥發豔麗潤質感。這是爲何有時二者會被混淆之故。 中國的核心疆域內缺乏優質的貴重寶礦，所以在元、明之前，除了閃玉外，只有一些半寶石類用作裝飾品的質材。石英家族中的玉髓、瑪瑙，是最早被選用的半寶石類。展品中有不少新石器時代至漢代的玉髓、瑪瑙製品，晶粒粗顯易見的就是水晶。可因有各種離子存在，或受輻射等因素而呈現各色色澤。清宮中常將透明帶色，可看見內部雕琢的寶石或半寶石泛稱爲「碧璽」。此次我們將曾被誤稱者與真正的碧璽並列展出，以供比對。 考古資料證明，漢代中晚期就有青金石與琥珀製品，推測礦源分別來自今日阿富汗與緬甸。中世紀以後波羅的海的琥珀亦進入中土。清宮中寶石種類甚多，多來自緬甸、泰國等東南亞地區，此間展出的紅色寶石經鑑定有：紅寶石、紅尖晶石、紅碧璽、紅玉髓等，未展出的庫藏還有紅�addr榴石。清宮對寶石鑲嵌的處理，與印度、土耳其等傳統不同，故特選二件異域文物以供比對。

walls.ex: 其他展區
walls.text: 西方對東方的想像 Western Imaginations of the East 十七世紀時，去過東方的傳教士以文字及銅版畫介紹亞洲，市場上也流通著東方的茶葉、瓷器等貿易品。神祕的東方更為十八世紀尋找奇幻感的洛可可藝術家帶來豐富的素材。不論是中國花鳥繪畫、亭台樓閣，甚至是想像的中國皇帝及市井小民，都是流行的題材，創造了夢境般的歐洲中國風裝飾圖樣。這座東方花園裡的圖案，即是取材自法國洛可可藝術家讓‧畢勒芒（Jean Pillement，1728-1808）創作的中國風圖繪。 In the 17th century, Europeans learned about Asian cultures through texts and engravings recorded by missionaries as well as Asian tea and porcelain in markets. The mysterious Oriental became a subject of interest for Rococo artists. Chinese bird-and-flower paintings, pagodas, and pavilions, even Chinese emperors and local figures became their sources of inspirations. The result is the creation of a fantastical and extremely decorative style of Chinoiserie. The Oriental garden scene in the gallery is based on the Chinoiserie works of French Rococo artist, Jean Pillement (1728-1808). 讓‧畢勒芒 絲綢繪畫上的中國主題 1760年 Jean Pillement Chinese Subjects Used in Painting of Silks (1760) 美國紐約大都會博物館藏 ©The Metropolitan Museum of Art, New York, USA

walls.ex: 其他展區
walls.text: 布雪的中國市集 1742年 Boucher's Chinese Fair (1742) 法蘭索瓦‧布雪（François Boucher，1703-1770）是洛可可繪畫的代表。在法國國王路易十五的宮廷擔任畫師，受到龐巴杜夫人推崇。他除了為賽弗爾瓷器設計紋飾，也設計了許多裝飾在牆壁上的壁掛毯。這幅輸出的圖像是布雪以想像的中國市集設計的壁掛毯。畫面裡的人物穿著奇特的服裝，市場上隨處可見東方的瓷器、織品及異域風情的動物。 François Boucher (1703-1770) was a representative of the Rococo painters. As a court painter for King Louis XV and a favorite of Madame de Pompadour, his works varied from designing patterns for Sèvres porcelain to decorative tapestries. This image is from a tapestry designed by Boucher. It shows an imaginary chinese marketplace. There are figures dressing in peculiar costumes, Asian porcelain and textiles, and exotic animals. 原作收藏於荷蘭阿姆斯特丹荷蘭國家博物館 © Rijksmuseum, Amsterdam, Netherlands

walls.ex: 其他展區
walls.text: 東方對西方的好奇 Eastern Interest in the West 十七、十八世紀，歐洲的工藝品經由貿易、餽贈等方式進入清代宮廷，引起皇帝極大的興趣。吸鼻煙成為新的流行，裝盛物從西洋鼻煙盒改成了鼻煙壺。西洋琺瑯及玻璃在宮廷作坊開發出新的顏色，西洋人物和風景也成了陶瓷上的裝飾題材。西洋鐘錶及帶有彩色寶石的配件成為帝后喜愛的飾品。各種精巧可愛的舶來品則被珍藏在多寶格。 想一想，你是用什麼方法認識世界的呢？ European art and crafts were brought into the Qing court in the 17th and 18th centuries. Some were diplomatic gifts while others were imported goods. Snuffing became a new trend but the containers went from snuffboxes to snuff bottles. Qing artisans developed new colors for enamel and glass, and they would paint Western scenes on the objects as decorations. Emperors and empresses enjoyed western clocks as well as other bejeweled accessories. Various lovely foreign objects were collected in the curio-boxes. How do you explore and learn about the world?

walls.ex: 其他展區
walls.text: 硯友圈 Inkstone Enthusiast Community 明清士人結社聚會，談詩論政，書畫交流，愛硯者則品硯、藏硯，於硯上書銘刻字，以硯相贈。文人或精於斫硯，工匠亦熟知書文，硯台與他們的仕宦生涯、人際交遊、生命經歷相交相纏。即使事過境遷，硯台依舊，刻記著過往的人事遞嬗。 During the Ming and Qing dynasties, literati would form social gatherings to discuss poetry, politics, and engage in calligraphy and painting exchanges. Those who had a fondness for inkstones would appreciate and collect them, inscribe words or dedications on the inkstone, and exchange these inkstones as gifts. Scholars skilled in carving inkstones would collaborate with artisans well-versed in literature, intertwining the inkstone with their scholarly careers, social connections, and life experiences. Even as times changed, the inkstone remained, bearing witness to the people and events of the past.

walls.ex: 其他展區
walls.text: 雙硯與色墨 Dual Inkstones and Colored Ink 批注公文或圈點書籍時，常用紅字，故雙硯應運而生。而色墨常用以寫經、作畫，或明清科舉閱卷及批閱重大刑案時，分別以藍、紫等各色，代表各職司的官員。 When annotating official documents or highlighting books, red ink is commonly used. As a result, dual inkstones came into existence. Colored ink, on the other hand, is often employed in writing scriptures, creating artwork, or during the Ming and Qing dynasties' imperial examination paper reading, as well as the reviewing of significant criminal cases. Different colors such as blue and purple were used to represent officials from various government departments. 墨有五色 The Five Colors of Ink 「運墨而五色具」，是指墨色蘊含濃、淡、乾、濕等的豐富變化，而最好的墨色是淡而不暗，黑而不滯，光亮而有神，其關鍵在於良墨，是現成墨汁所無法企及。 The rich variations in ink colors encompass qualities like thickness, lightness, dryness, and wetness. The best ink color is one that is light but not dull, black yet not stagnant, bright and lively. The key lies in high-quality ink, which cannot be achieved by simply using ready-made ink solutions.

walls.ex: 其他展區
walls.text: 早期硯式 Early Inkstone Styles 考古出土最早可以稱為「硯」的是戰國至漢代的板硯，多取自天然石塊，表面平整，打磨光滑，並附有研石。東漢流行動物蓋鈕的三足石硯，六朝隋唐墓經常出土多足的圓形硯，並成為宋元以後仿古硯的形制之一。 The earliest artifacts that can be called "inkstones" in archaeological discoveries are the slab inkstones from the Warring States Period to the Han Dynasty. They were mostly made from natural stones, with a smooth and polished surface, and equipped with a grinding stone. During the Eastern Han Dynasty, three-legged inkstones made of stone materials with animal-shaped lids gained popularity. In tombs from the Six Dynasties, Sui Dynasty, and Tang Dynasty, multi-legged round inkstones were constantly unearthed, and they later became one of the traditional forms of inkstones imitated during the Song and Yuan Dynasties.

walls.ex: 其他展區
walls.text: 硯材的遐想 The Imagination of Inkstone Materials 蘇軾說，他十二歲在住處附近玩掘地遊戲時，曾經撿到一塊適合做為硯台的石頭。如此看來，在「易發墨」的基本要求下，硯材具有極大的可能性，若又符合美石的條件，就更理想了。您想過醫療用的脈枕和屋頂的瓦當也可以作硯嗎？而美麗的石頭，如玉、瑪瑙，又如何化身為硯呢？ Scholar Su Shi said that when he was twelve years old, while playing, he once found a stone that was suitable for use as an inkstone. In this regard, the Inkstone materials have great possibilities, considering the basic requirements of "easy ink dispersion." It would be even more ideal if the material also meets the criteria of beautiful stones. Have you ever considered using the wrist cushion for Chinese medical treatment or the roof tiles of a house as inkstones? And how can beautiful stones, such as jade or agate, transform into inkstones?

walls.ex: 其他展區
walls.text: 玉質細滑，適合製硯嗎？ Is a Smooth Jade Suitable for Making Inkstones? 硯以易於發墨又不傷筆墨為佳，硯材宜軟硬適中、粗細恰當，玉質堅韌細緻，較適合研磨硃墨，或硯堂局部不打磨，以增加摩擦力。 For inkstones, the ideal qualities include easy ink release without damaging the brush or ink. The material of the inkstone should strike a balance between softness and hardness, and an appropriate level of coarseness. Jade, being both sturdy and delicate, is better suited for grinding cinnabar ink or for leaving certain parts of the inkstone unpolished to increase friction during use.

walls.ex: 其他展區
walls.text: 有名的硯材 Reputed Inkstone Materials 澄泥，以澄洗過的細泥加工燒製而成，是早期常見硯材，以山西絳州的澄泥硯為代表。宋明以來，天然硯石，以山東紅絲石、甘肅洮河石、安徽歙石及廣東端石最負盛名，各地亦不乏佳硯。 Chengni is a type of inkstone material made from finely-washed clay and processed through firing. It was a commonly used material in early times, with the Chengni inkstones from Jiangzhou, Shanxi province being a representative example. Starting from the Song and Ming Dynasties, natural inkstone materials gained their prominence. Among them, the most renowned are the Hongsi Stone from Shandong, the Tao River Stone from Gansu, the She Stone from Anhui, and the Duan Stone from Guangdong. Many other regions in China also produced excellent inkstones. 臺灣硯[石] ... 清嘉慶年... 道光至同... 曾經採拾... 質灰黑... 數例證之... Taiwanese... During the... mentioned... for making... (1848-1864... stones from... Inkstone." Th... metallic min... Taiwanese in...

walls.ex: 其他展區
walls.text: 臺灣硯石 Taiwanese Inkstones 清嘉慶年間文獻記載，濁水溪一帶產溪石，可為硯。道光至同治年間（1848-1864）在臺灣任官的王衢，曾經採拾宜蘭蘭坑羅溪石，製成《露布硯》一方，石質灰黑，帶金屬礦砂，是現存臺灣十九世紀用硯的少數例證之一。 During the Jiaqing reign of the Qing Dynasty, historical records mentioned that stones from the Zhusui River area could be used for making inkstones. From the Daoguang to Tongzhi reigns (1848-1864), Wang Qu, an official stationed in Taiwan, collected stones from the Luo River in Lankeng, Yilan, and crafted the "Lu-bu Inkstone." These stones were greyish-black in color and contained metallic minerals. They stand as a rare example of 19th-century Taiwanese inkstones that have survived to the present day.

walls.ex: 其他展區
walls.text: 硯式的完備 The Maturation of Inkstone Styles 隨著宋代科舉制度的成熟，社會上形成龐大的士人群體，這群廣大的用硯人口為日後硯石的發展奠定了基礎。1987年以前台灣中、小學生都是用毛筆寫作文，常用長方形硯台磨墨，這種平底的硯式源於宋代硯底挖空的「抄手硯」，您想知道他們是如何演變的嗎？ With the maturity of the imperial examination system during the Song Dynasty, a large group of scholars emerged in society. This extensive population of literati laid the foundation for the development of inkstones in the future. Prior to 1987, students in primary and secondary schools in Taiwan used brushes to write compositions and often used rectangular inkstones to grind ink. This flat-bottomed inkstone style originated from the handle inkstone of the Song Dynasty, which featured a hollowed-out base. Would you like to know how those inkstones evolved?

walls.ex: 其他展區
walls.text: 風字硯 Inkstone in the Shape of Chinese Character "Feng (wind)" 硯底有足，硯尾抬高，方便墨汁向後溜流至硯首，墨池和硯堂沒有明顯的區隔。硯尾寬，硯首窄，硯面俯視為梯形，其前身為箕形硯。 The inkstone has feet at its base, and the tail of the inkstone is raised, making it convenient for the ink to flow backward towards the inkstone's head. There is no distinct separation between the inkwell and the inkstone platform. The tail of the inkstone is wide, while the head is narrow. When viewed from above, the inkstone's surface appears trapezoidal. The predecessor of this inkstone was the winnower-shaped inkstone.

walls.ex: 其他展區
walls.text: 抄手硯 Handle Inkstone 抄手硯是宋代硯式的主流，硯底挖空，墨池陡降，硯邊明顯，硯形平整，其前身為風字硯。明代抄手硯的硯面平整，硯底常刻意以圓柱保留端石特有的石眼。 The handle inkstone was a popular style of inkstone during the Song dynasty. It is characterized by a hollowed-out base, a steeply sloping ink reservoir, prominent edges, and a flat shape. Its predecessor was the inkstone in the shape of Chinese character "Feng (wind)". During the Ming dynasty, the handle inkstone had a smooth surface, and the base was often intentionally carved into a cylindrical shape to preserve the distinctive stone-eye feature.

walls.ex: 其他展區
walls.text: 長方形硯 Rectangular Inkstone 硯底無足亦無側牆，重視硯面墨池與硯堂的各種形式，如鳳池、括囊、井田硯等，並且賦予各種意涵，甚至不分墨池與硯堂，呈現硯石本身自然的紋理，是明清硯的主流之一。 The rectangular inkstone is characterized by a base without feet or side walls. It features various forms of the ink surface, ink reservoir, and inkstone platform, such as the Phoenix pond (feng ci), enclosing pouch (gua nang), well-field inkstone (jing tian), and so forth. Each form carries different meanings and even blurs the distinction between the ink reservoir and the inkstone platform. This style showcases the natural texture of the inkstone itself and was one of the mainstream inkstone styles during the Ming and Qing Dynasties.

walls.ex: 硯特展｜與硯為友
walls.text: 圭形硯 玉圭，是祭器的禮器，也是諸侯朝覲天子時手中的持物，代表統治地方的權杖，因此圭形硯有出仕為官之意。 Inkstone with Gui Decoration The "yu gui" has significance as a ritual object used in ceremonies and was also held by feudal lords when they paid visits to the emperor. It symbolized their authority over their territories. The design of an inkstone in the shape of a "yu gui" carries the meaning of pursuing a career in government service or officialdom. 井田硯 井田是指將田地畫分成井字形的方塊，士人以筆墨為生，將硯稱為「硯田」，以筆耕田，或戲謔自比為耕田的牛。 Well-field Inkstone Well-field Inkstone refers to an inkstone design that is reminiscent of the layout of land distribution in a checkerboard pattern. Scholars who made their living through writing likened their inkstone to their "tian," meaning fields. They humorously associated, using their pens to "cultivate" their "fields" of knowledge or jokingly comparing themselves to oxen tilling the land. This metaphor highlights the significance of their writing tools in their intellectual endeavors.

walls.ex: 硯特展｜與硯為友
walls.text: 子石硯 宋人米芾愛硯也愛石，是著眼於天然石本身的詭譎瑰麗，而明代對奇石的愛好又多了仙山、隱居的意涵。畫中庭園的疊石假山，四面環水，猶如孤島，與世隔離。 Zishi Inkstone Zishi Inkstone refers to an inkstone design that imitates the appearance of piled rocks in a garden or rockery. During the Song Dynasty, Mi Fu, a renowned scholar and artist, had a deep appreciation for both inkstones and natural rocks. He focused on the enchanting and intricate beauty of natural stones. In the Ming Dynasty, the love for peculiar rocks took on connotations of fairy mountains and seclusion. Paintings often depicted rockeries in gardens, surrounded by water on all sides, resembling isolated islands that are separated from the rest of the world. Peaks of Gathered ... 聚石

walls.ex: 硯特展｜與硯為友
walls.text: 魚式硯 水中悠遊的魚兒，具有「如魚得水」的美好寓意，清代取自東北龍興之地的魚化石又另有一層飲水思源、永不忘祖的警惕之意。 Fish-shaped Inkstone Fish-shaped Inkstone refers to an inkstone design inspired by the graceful movements of fish swimming in water. The imagery of fish in water carries the beautiful connotation of being in one's element, which means feeling comfortable and at ease. During the Qing Dynasty, the design of fish-shaped inkstones drew from fossilized fish found in the homeland of Manchurian. This design also carries the metaphorical significance of "drinking water and remembering its source," serving as a reminder to never forget one's roots and heritage.

walls.ex: 硯特展｜與硯為友
walls.text: 蕉葉、枯木與竹的造形硯 「雨打芭蕉」與「蕉葉題詩」是文學的浪漫，枯木老幹是擷取自大自然的強勁生命力，竹子則具有許多美好的意涵。竹筍可作佳餚，竹林清雅怡人，而像徵君子德性的竹節，更成為製硯發揮的重點，無論縱觀或橫視之，均可賞、可用。 Inkstones in shapes of Banana Leaf, Withered Wood and Bamboo "Raindrops on Banana Leaves" and "Inscribing Poems on Banana Leaves" are romantic literary expressions. "Withered Wood" draws from the strength of nature's resilience, portraying the tenacity of life even in its mature stage. "Bamboo" symbolizes lots of goodness. Bamboo shoots are enjoyed as delicacies, bamboo groves create a tranquil atmosphere. The symbolism of the Bamboo nodes, representing the virtue of a noble person, becomes a key element in crafting inkstones. Regardless of the perspective, these designs are both visually appealing and functional.

walls.ex: 硯特展｜與硯為友
walls.text: 與硯為友 Befriending the Inkstones 硯原是文人賴以為生的工具，文人與硯建立良好的友誼之後，洗硯、製硯盒，以愛護、照顧他們；為硯傳形拓模，撰寫硯譜，留下記錄；還有愛硯人一起賞硯、談硯，形成硯交圈。您會為自己珍愛的收藏品做些什麼？ The inkstone was originally a vital tool for literati, and after establishing a strong bond with their inkstones, scholars would wash them, craft inkstone boxes, and care for them with love and attention. They would create rubbings and write inkstone catalogues to document their inkstones. Apart from this, scholars would gather with fellow inkstone enthusiasts to appreciate and talk about inkstones, forming a community of inkstone experts. What would you do for your cherished collections?

walls.ex: 硯特展｜與硯為友
walls.section: 硯展（與硯為友 / 文化記憶）
walls.text: 文化記憶 Cultural Memory 硯石堅固，猶如碑石，可歷代相傳，形成文化記憶的載體。硯上文化名人的刻銘，引發今月曾經照古人之嘆，而以蘭亭修禊與羲之換鵝旳故事為題材，或是表現蘇軾雋永的文學傳奇，更是硯石最愛傳誦的內涵。 Inkstones, with their durable nature akin to stone tablets, can be passed down through generations and become carriers of cultural memory. The engraved inscriptions of cultural figures on inkstones evoke admiration and reflection on the past. Themes such as the Orchid Pavilion Gathering and Writing Scriptures for Geese serve as inspirations while portraying the timeless literary legends of Su Shi.

walls.ex: 硯特展｜與硯為友
walls.text: 硯的珍藏 Preserving the Inkstone 硯不可一日不洗滌，洗硯除了去垢，還要以清水養石潤。為了避免燥石，收藏硯的硯盒不宜用金屬材質，大多為漆盒或木質，量身訂製。再者，凡經手、過目的佳硯，沒有相機也無妨，繪圖、模拓，加上文字描述，留下永恆的記錄。 An inkstone should never go unwashed for a day. Cleaning the inkstone with clear water not only removes impurities but also nourishes the stone. To prevent the inkstone from drying out, it is advisable to store it in a dedicated inkstone box made of materials such as lacquer or wood rather than metal. These boxes are often custom-made to fit the inkstone precisely. Furthermore, for cherished inkstones that have been appreciated and examined, much have been passed down even without a camera. Drawing, making rubbings, and adding written descriptions can create enduring records, preserving their beauty and significance for an eternity.

walls.ex: 硯特展｜與硯為友
walls.text: 呂留良（1629-1683）浙江海寧人，明代淮莊王女婿王煐的後代，家境富足，自幼喜愛硯石，與黃宗羲、吳之振等人為硯友，以「友硯堂」為堂號。 Lyu Liuliang (1629-1683), a native of Haining in Zhejiang, was a descendant of Lyu Ying, the son-in-law of Huaizhuangwang during the Ming Dynasty. Coming from an affluent family, he had a deep passion for inkstones from a young age. He shared his enthusiasm with fellow inkstone aficionados such as Huang Zongxi and Wu Zhizhen. He adopted the hall name "You Yan Tang", which translates to "Hall of Inkstone Friends."

walls.ex: 硯特展｜與硯為友
walls.text: 林佶、余甸、黃任和林涪雲等人是清代康熙年間福州地方附近的愛硯人。他們有人藏書豐富，又或精於賞硯、擅長硯銘，還有篆刻家、刻字名家等。因常將佳石交付浙江斫硯名家顧二娘，往來密切，形成藏硯圈。 Lin Ji, Yu Dian, Huang Ren, and Lin Fuyun were inkstone enthusiasts from the vicinity of Fuzhou during the Kangxi reign of the Qing Dynasty. Some of them possessed extensive book collections, excelled in appreciating inkstones, were skilled in deciphering inscriptions on them, and included seal engravers and renowned engravers among their ranks. They often entrusted fine stones to the accomplished Fuzhou inkstone carver Gu Erniang in Zhejiang, maintaining a close connection and forming a community of inkstone collectors.

walls.ex: 硯特展｜與硯為友
walls.text: 天生麗質—硯石的紋理 Inherent Beauty: The Texture of Inkstones 硯石本身的紋理，稱為石品，是愛硯者賞硯的重點之一，因紋理的形貌有各種名稱，以下簡要介紹端石與歙石的主要紋理。 The natural texture of the inkstone, known as the "stone quality", is one of the key aspects appreciated by inkstone aficionados. The various patterns in the texture have distinct names. Below is a brief introduction to the main textures found in Duan inkstones and She inkstones.

walls.ex: 硯特展｜與硯為友
walls.text: 端石 Duan Stone 石品大略分為三類：深色的火捺，淺色的魚腦凍、蕉葉白，以及青黃點狀的青花、石眼。 The stone quality can be roughly categorized into three types: the dark-fired strokes, the light-colored fish-brain jelly and banana-leaf white, and the blue and yellow spot-like blue and stone eyes.

walls.ex: 硯特展｜與硯為友
walls.text: 歙石 She Stone 以安徽南部古稱歙州而得名，硯石天然紋理美妙異常，因石質疏密交疊而形成，以下擇四例說明之。 Named after the ancient name of Shezhou in southern Anhui, She stone possesses a remarkably unique and beautiful natural texture. This texture is formed by the interweaving of dense and sparse stone layers. The following are four examples to illustrate this unique quality.

walls.ex: 青銅兵器與秦漢銅器｜祀與戎
walls.text: 帶鉤，約始於春秋而盛於戰國。質材多樣、鑲嵌多方，在貴族的衣帶上，有精彩的開發：嵌錯金、銀、銅及綠松石等，構成小中見大的細緻景觀。 Belt hooks appeared in the Spring and Autumn period and turned very popular during the Warring State period. They are of diverse materials and with all kinds of inlay from gold, silver to bronze and turquoise. A small ornamental addition greatly enhanced the look of belts and ribbons on an aristocrat. 帶鉤 Belt Hooks

walls.ex: 青銅兵器與秦漢銅器｜祀與戎
walls.text: 戰國銅鏡紋飾常見有山字紋、菱形紋、連弧紋、羽翅紋、龍紋、夔紋、狩獵紋等，往往承襲自東周銅器紋樣而加以轉化、然後自成系統，是銅鏡發展史上的第一度高峰。 The common patterns decorating the bronze mirrors from the Warring States period include "shan-zi" (a pattern similar to the character "mountain"), lozenges, connected arcs, feathers, dragons, kui dragons, hunting scenes, and so on. Most were the modified versions based upon those on the bronze vessels of Eastern Zhou and then evolved into a system of their own, achieving the first peak in the history of bronze mirrors. 銅鏡 Mirrors

others.caption: 展廳全景，前景透明壓克力展台上有東方瓶、缽與珠寶飾件，背景人群與遠處展櫃。
展櫃全景，可見環形珠寶飾件、花卉胸針與背景設計圖框，多名觀眾剪影。
酒紅展櫃中懸吊的大型梨形黑色物件（疑為珍珠或工藝展示道具），下方為一金屬碟形件。
陰天下國立故宮博物院正館建築外觀與前方廣場。
由故宮高處俯瞰前庭草坪、牌坊與遠方山景建築。
故宮正館前廣場指示牌與建築外觀。
故宮石造平台與拱橋下方一件青銅鼎造型雕塑。
四幅藏傳佛教唐卡／經板畫局部展示（佛龕、本尊、瓶飾），含少量藏文未能辨識
石雕狛犬／獅子坐像（花崗岩），蹲踞含珠，背景可見神獸繪畫展品
展覽圖錄《神獸現形：文物中的奇幻生物》版權頁，手持書本翻拍
展覽圖錄書封，封面為青花龍紋
投影螢幕畫面，展示獸紋玉劍首並打字幕
投影畫面，展示杉原厚吉錯視作品「海波草浪」雙視角
展櫃全景，左側展台立架展示玉劍璲與圓形玉劍首，右側展示玉劍珌
手機螢幕截圖：故宮特展問卷提交完成頁與桌布下載連結
展間牆面的大型中國風壁掛毯複製圖（布雪式中國市集場景）與長椅，無文字說明。
八吉祥（寶傘、雙魚、寶瓶、妙蓮、白螺、吉祥結、勝利幢、法輪）圖卡立牌。
龍藏經特展觀眾意見回饋與手機桌布下載分享圖（含 QR Code）。
